seoTitle: Rawdon Island vs Rawdon Vale — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Rawdon Island and Rawdon Vale. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Rawdon Island vs Rawdon Vale: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Rawdon Island and Rawdon Vale.
heading: Rawdon Island vs Rawdon Vale — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Rawdon Island, New South Wales and Rawdon Vale, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Rawdon Vale has a lower overall climate risk score (43/100) compared to Rawdon Island (48/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Rawdon Island or Rawdon Vale safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Rawdon Island has a flood risk score of 58/100, while Rawdon Vale scores 49/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Rawdon Island scores 39/100 for bushfire risk. Rawdon Vale scores 41/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Rawdon Island or Rawdon Vale?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Rawdon Vale has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.68% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
